stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Before 1862, the US federal government had no general system for granting land to ordinary settlers.
  • Public land was often sold in large parcels that only wealthy buyers could afford.
  • This kept land ownership concentrated among a small group of buyers.
  • The 1862 Homestead Act allowed adult citizens to claim 160 acres of public land in exchange for living on and farming it for five years.
  • By 1900, hundreds of thousands of settlers had filed claims under the act.
stem: The student wants to present the significance of the Homestead Act to an audience unfamiliar with the history of US land policy. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: By 1900, hundreds of thousands of settlers had filed claims under the 1862 Homestead Act.
Before 1862, the US federal government had no general system for granting land to ordinary settlers.

The 1862 Homestead Act was significant because, by allowing adult citizens to claim 160 acres of public land in exchange for living on and farming it for five years, it opened land ownership beyond the wealthy buyers who had dominated earlier sales.
Public land sold before 1862 was often offered in large parcels that only wealthy buyers could afford.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence presents the significance of the Homestead Act to an unfamiliar audience by explaining what the act did and how it broke from the earlier pattern of land ownership being concentrated among wealthy buyers.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence reports the act’s scale but doesn’t present its significance. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence describes the pre-1862 situation but doesn’t present the significance of the act itself. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes earlier land sales but doesn’t present the significance of the Homestead Act.
